title: I Can’t Stop Buying Microsoft Because It’s Turning This AI Bottleneck Into a Moat
excerpt: Microsoft is down big, capex is exploding, and critics are calling AI infrastructure the fastest-depreciating asset in tech history. So why does every dip fe...
content_html:
Alex Sirois

Thu, July 23, 2026 at 10:49 AM CDT 4 min read

Quick Read

  • MSFT dropped 17% year to date but beat EPS four consecutive quarters, with AI revenue hitting a $37 billion annual run rate.

  • Microsoft turns the AI power bottleneck into a moat by funding nuclear and SMR projects, securing capacity rivals cannot source on demand.

  • The $627 billion contracted backlog, up 99% year over year, directly counters concerns about $31 billion quarterly capex and two-year chip obsolescence cycles.

  • Act now: the analyst who called NVIDIA in 2010 just named his top 10 AI stocks — and Microsoft didn't make the cut. Grab the names FREE today.

I keep buying Microsoft because it is the only hyperscaler I trust to own both ends of the AI supply chain: the software everyone already pays for, and the electrons that will decide who actually gets to run the models. That combination is why my finger keeps hitting the buy button, and it is why the recent drawdown feels like a gift rather than a warning.

Here is the setup. Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) is down 17.39% year to date and 21.39% over the past year, yet the business underneath it just posted its fourth consecutive EPS beat with $4.27 against a $4.07 estimate. Revenue climbed 18.3% year over year to $82.89 billion. The market is punishing capex. I am accumulating.

The Three Data Points That Keep Me Buying

First, the demand signal. Commercial remaining performance obligations reached $627 billion, up 99%. That is contracted, signed, non-cancellable future revenue that nearly doubled in a year. Azure grew 40%, and the AI business alone crossed a $37 billion annual run rate, up 123% year over year. Satya Nadella framed it plainly on the call: "Our AI business surpassed an annual revenue run rate of $37 billion, up 123% year-over-year."

Second, the quality of the compounding. Return on equity sits at 33.28%, operating margin at 45.62%, gross margin at 68.82%. Debt to equity is 0.176 and interest coverage runs 53.89x. This is a fortress funding a build-out. Shareholders got $12.7 billion returned in a single quarter, up 32% year over year.

Act now: the analyst who called NVIDIA in 2010 just named his top 10 AI stocks — and Microsoft didn't make the cut. Grab the names FREE today.

Third, and this is the part that turns a good business into a moat: energy. By aggressively funding nuclear restarts, SMRs, and grid-permitting AI, Microsoft turns energy from an external existential risk into a proprietary moat, ensuring its data centers stay powered while turning the energy transition into a software-driven profit center. The LBNL projection has data centers consuming between 6.7% and 12% of U.S. electricity by 2028. Power is the bottleneck now. Microsoft is buying its way to the front of that line.

content_html_zh:
亚历克斯·西罗伊斯

2026 年 7 月 23 日星期四上午 10:49 CDT 阅读时间 4 分钟

快速阅读

  • MSFT 今年迄今下跌 17%，但连续四个季度超过每股收益，人工智能收入年增长率达到 370 亿美元。
  • 微软通过资助核能和SMR项目，将人工智能电力瓶颈变成护城河，确保竞争对手无法按需采购的能力。
  • 6270 亿美元的合同积压订单同比增长 99%，直接抵消了对 310 亿美元季度资本支出和两年芯片报废周期的担忧。
  • 立即行动：2010 年给 NVIDIA 打电话的分析师刚刚列出了他心目中的十大人工智能股票，而微软并未上榜。 今天免费获取名字。

我继续购买微软，因为它是我信任的唯一一家拥有人工智能供应链两端的超大规模企业：每个人都已经付费购买的软件，以及将决定谁真正可以运行模型的电子设备。 这种结合就是为什么我的手指不断按下购买按钮，也是为什么最近的回撤感觉像是一份礼物而不是警告。

这是设置。 微软（纳斯达克股票代码：MSFT）今年迄今下跌了 17.39%，较去年下跌了 21.39%，但其旗下业务刚刚公布每股收益连续第四次好于预期，每股收益为 4.27 美元，而预期为 4.07 美元。 收入同比增长 18.3% 至 828.9 亿美元。 市场正在惩罚资本支出。 我正在积累。

让我继续购买的三个数据点

一是需求信号。 商业剩余履约义务达到 6270 亿美元，增长 99%。 这是合同规定的、已签署的、不可取消的未来收入，在一年内几乎翻了一番。 Azure 增长了 40%，仅 AI 业务的年运行率就突破了 370 亿美元，同比增长 123%。 萨蒂亚·纳德拉 (Satya Nadella) 在电话会议上明确表示：“我们的人工智能业务年收入超过 370 亿美元，同比增长 123%。”

二是复配质量。 股本回报率为33.28%，营业利润率为45.62%，毛利率为68.82%。 债务股本比为 0.176，利息保障倍数为 53.89 倍。 这是一座资助扩建的堡垒。 单季度股东回报达 127 亿美元，同比增长 32%。

立即行动：2010 年给 NVIDIA 打电话的分析师刚刚列出了他心目中的十大人工智能股票，而微软并未上榜。 今天免费获取名字。

第三，这就是将好生意变成护城河的部分：能源。 通过积极资助核重启、SMR 和电网允许的人工智能，微软将能源从外部存在风险转变为专有护城河，确保其数据中心保持供电，同时将能源转型转变为软件驱动的利润中心。 劳伦斯伯克利国家实验室 (LBNL) 预测，到 2028 年，数据中心将消耗美国电力的 6.7% 至 12%。 现在电力是瓶颈。 微软正在通过收购来跻身这一行列的前列。
